q:
Are you allowed to overtake the motorcycle on the left at this intersection?

A. Yes.
B. Yes, if you leave sufficient lateral distance.
C. No.
e:
LESSON 16
At intersections where no traffic signs regulate priority, or where this road sign is present and therefore the priority to the right applies, it is forbidden to overtake on the left:
a vehicle with a trailer,
a vehicle with more than two wheels,
or a motor vehicle with two wheels.
A cyclist is not a motor vehicle, so you are allowed to overtake them. You may also overtake a personal mobility device.

or a motor vehicle with two wheels.
A cyclist is not a motor vehicle, so you are allowed to overtake them. You may also overtake a personal mobility device.

How much lateral distance must the driver keep here when overtaking the moped rider?

A. 1 metre.
B. 1.5 metres.
C. Sufficient.
e:
LESSON 15
When overtaking a cyclist or a moped rider within built-up areas, you must keep 1 metre of lateral distance.
Outside built-up areas, the distance must be at least 1.5 metres.

A. Yes, always.
B. Yes, if you leave one metre of lateral safety distance.

On a raised area, you may only overtake a cyclist on the left.

q:
A roadway divided into lanes outside a built-up area.
May the red truck overtake the white car on the left?
e:
LESSON 16
This prohibition sign indicates that trucks or combinations with a maximum authorised mass exceeding 3500 kg may not overtake:
- a combination of vehicles, or
- any vehicle with more than two wheels.
The no-overtaking rule applies:
- up to and including the next intersection, or
- ends earlier if you see the “end of overtaking prohibition” sign.
A car driver may therefore overtake a truck on the left after this sign.
A bus may also overtake, since it is not a truck.

A. Yes.
B. No.

LESSON 16
On a two-way road, you may overtake a car that is overtaking a moped or a motorcycle.
BUT: you may not overtake a car that is itself overtaking another car.

Which vehicles are you allowed to overtake on the left if it's safe?

When the red lights flash alternately, you must stop and you are not allowed to enter the level crossing.

q:

A. Yes, because a truck is not a passenger car.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 16
The red circular prohibition sign indicates that it is forbidden for drivers to:
- overtake a combination of vehicles, or
- overtake any vehicle with more than two wheels.
The no-overtaking rule applies:
- up to and including the next intersection, or
- ends earlier if you see the “end of overtaking prohibition” sign.
A car driver may therefore overtake a two-wheeled motorcycle,
but the motorcyclist may not overtake a car, because a car has more than two wheels.

C. 1.5 metres.
e:
LESSON 15

The distance kept by this car is therefore insufficient.

q:
A one-way street within a built-up area.
May the blue car continue driving on this lane?

A. Yes.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 15
Within a built-up area, drivers may choose the lane that best suits their destination, provided that:
- the road is divided into lanes, and
- the road is located in a built-up area.
This is allowed in two cases:
- on one-way roads with lanes;
- on two-way roads divided into four or more lanes, with at least two lanes for each direction.

- on one-way roads with lanes;
- on two-way roads divided into four or more lanes, with at least two lanes for each direction.

Drivers are allowed to follow the lane they prefer within built-up areas:
. on a one-way road divided into lanes,
. on a two-way road, with four or more lanes of which at least two are for each direction.

At level crossings indicated by this sign, overtaking on the left is prohibited for: a horse-drawn vehicle; a vehicle with more than two wheels; or a two-wheeled motor vehicle, except when barriers are present or when the lunar white light is on.

q:
You are approaching a crossroads and you see one of these signs.
At which intersection can you overtake a cyclist on the left?

A. Only at intersection 1.
B. Only at intersection 2.
C. At intersection 1 and 2.
e: At intersections where there are no signs that control the priority, or where board 1 is, and thus the priority of the right, it is forbidden to overtake animals in harness or a vehicle on more than two wheels or a two-wheel motor vehicle. (A cyclist is not a motor vehicle and may you catch up with it).

LESSON 16
At intersections where you have priority, you may overtake other drivers on the left.
- Priority at the next intersection.
- Priority road.
- Green traffic light.

q: May the car overtake the truck on the left?
e:
LESSON 16
This prohibition sign indicates that trucks or combinations with a maximum authorised mass exceeding 3500 kg may not overtake:
- a combination of vehicles, or
- any vehicle with more than two wheels.
The no-overtaking rule applies:
- up to and including the next intersection, or
- ends earlier if you see the “end of overtaking prohibition” sign.
A car driver may therefore overtake a truck on the left after this sign.
A bus may also overtake, since it is not a truck.
clean_q: May the car overtake the truck on the left?
clean_e:

- ends earlier if you see the “end of overtaking prohibition” sign.
A car driver may therefore overtake a truck on the left after this sign.
A bus may also overtake, since it is not a truck.

q:
Enter a number.
You want to overtake a car. What do you do first?

1. You put your turn signal on.
2. You look over your shoulder.
3. You look in the outside mirror.
4. You look in the rear view mirror.
e: Always check in the rear-view mirror first to make sure you are not being overtaken yourself.

q:
May the car overtake the horse-drawn cart on the left?

A. Yes.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 16
The red circular prohibition sign indicates that it is forbidden for drivers to:
- overtake a combination of vehicles, or
- overtake any vehicle with more than two wheels.
The no-overtaking rule applies:
- up to and including the next intersection, or
- ends earlier if you see the “end of overtaking prohibition” sign.
A car driver may therefore overtake a two-wheeled motorcycle,
but the motorcyclist may not overtake a car, because a car has more than two wheels.

- ends earlier if you see the “end of overtaking prohibition” sign.
A car driver may therefore overtake a two-wheeled motorcycle,
but the motorcyclist may not overtake a car, because a car has more than two wheels.
